summary: Web Console V4 (popup/new tab JWT proxy) testing completed. Backend: 16/16 tests passed (100%). Frontend: compiles without errors, V4 button correctly implemented in WebConsoleTabs.js (lines 385-392 in ActiveConsole toolbar, lines 591-600 in QuickAccessItem).

issues: Minor React warning: 'Cannot update a component while rendering a different component' - not blocking

critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/console-v4/request-session correctly validates device_ip (400 if missing, 404 if not in DB)
JWT token generation uses correct payload structure (sid, dip, cid, usr, base, iat, exp)
Proxy endpoint correctly handles: invalid token (401), expired token (410 with HTML), valid requests
HTML rewriting correctly injects <base> tag and rewrites absolute URLs
Redirect Location headers are correctly rewritten to go through proxy
Sessions list and revoke endpoints work correctly with RBAC (viewer gets 403 on revoke)
V4 button has correct data-testid='web-console-popup-v4' in ActiveConsole toolbar
V4 button has correct data-testid='quick-popup-{ip}' in QuickAccessItem
openPopup function correctly calls /api/console-v4/request-session and opens window.open()
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - used existing device 192.168.1.8 for request-session tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Web Console V4 endpoints fully tested. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_web_console_v4.py covers: auth requirements, request-session validation (400/404), proxy with httpbin.org (JSON, HTML base injection, redirect rewriting), expired/invalid tokens, sessions list, revoke RBAC. Frontend V4 button is in WebConsoleTabs.js - visible when a Web Console session is active. Device 192.168.1.8 is in 'SOLO CONNECTOR' mode so direct console opening may require connector.
